title: stereochilus arunachalensis
intro: Stereochilus arunachalensis is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to the northeastern region of India, particularly in the state of Arunachal Pradesh. It is a type of terrestrial orchid, which is a flowering plant that grows in the ground and has adapted to survive in low-light conditions. The medicinal properties of Stereochilus arunachalensis have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent and treat various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, including fever, skin diseases, and respiratory problems, and is often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
